name: FINOS Architecture as Code (CALM) Monorepo
description: Architecture of the FINOS CALM (Common Architecture Language Model) open-source monorepo, including the CALM Hub backend, UI, CLI tooling, validation server, DevSecOps compliance platform, and desktop studio.

nodes.name: Architect / Developer
nodes.description: Software architect or developer who uses CALM tooling to model, validate, and manage architectural artifacts.

nodes.name: CALM Hub
nodes.description: Quarkus/Java REST API backend for storing, retrieving, and serving CALM namespaces, architectures, patterns, flows, standards, and controls; also exposes an embedded MCP endpoint at /mcp.

nodes.name: CALM Hub UI
nodes.description: React single-page application for browsing, visualizing, and managing CALM architectures; built and served as static resources embedded inside CALM Hub.

nodes.name: CALM Server
nodes.description: Express/Node.js REST API exposing CALM document validation (POST /calm/validate) and a health check (GET /health) as a standalone service.

nodes.name: CALM CLI
nodes.description: Node.js command-line tool providing generate, validate, template, docify, and init-ai commands for working with CALM architecture files.

nodes.name: MongoDB
nodes.description: Document database storing CALM namespaces, architectures, patterns, standards, flows, controls, decorators, and user-access records.

nodes.name: Keycloak
nodes.description: Keycloak OIDC/OAuth2 identity provider that issues and signs JWT tokens for authenticating users of CALM Hub.

nodes.name: CALM Guard
nodes.description: Next.js DevSecOps compliance web application that fetches CALM architectures from GitHub, validates them via calm-cli, and runs parallel AI analysis agents for compliance mapping, pipeline generation, and risk scoring.

nodes.name: CALM Studio
nodes.description: Tauri-based cross-platform desktop application for authoring and visualizing CALM architecture models with a graphical editor.

nodes.name: CALM Studio MCP Server
nodes.description: Standalone Model Context Protocol (MCP) server exposing CALM node, relationship, and architecture tools to AI agent clients via stdio or HTTP.

nodes.name: CALM VS Code Extension
nodes.description: VS Code extension providing CALM architecture authoring, live validation, diagnostics, and graph visualization within the editor.

nodes.name: GitHub API
nodes.description: External GitHub REST API used by CALM Guard to fetch CALM architecture JSON files from repositories and create pull requests with remediation changes.

nodes.name: Anthropic Claude
nodes.description: External Anthropic AI API providing the Claude Sonnet language model for AI-powered CALM architecture analysis in CALM Guard.

nodes.name: OpenAI GPT
nodes.description: External OpenAI API providing GPT-4o as an alternative AI provider for CALM architecture analysis in CALM Guard.

relationships.description: Architects and developers interact with CALM tooling to author, validate, visualize, and manage architecture models.
Sends HTTP REST requests to read and write namespaces, patterns, architectures, flows, decorators, and ADRs.
Performs the OIDC authorization code flow to authenticate users when secure mode is enabled.
Reads and writes all CALM namespace data, architectures, patterns, standards, flows, controls, and user-access records.
Retrieves OIDC public keys (JWKS) to validate JWT bearer tokens on incoming authenticated requests.
Fetches CALM architecture JSON files from GitHub repositories and creates pull requests with remediation changes.
Submits CALM architecture content to Claude Sonnet for AI-powered compliance mapping, pipeline generation, cloud infrastructure planning, and risk scoring.
Submits CALM architecture content to GPT-4o as an alternative AI provider when Anthropic is not configured.
Invokes calm-cli as a child process to validate CALM JSON documents server-side.
Fetches remote CALM patterns and schemas from CALM Hub when a --calm-hub-url is supplied.
Optionally loads remote CALM schemas from CALM Hub for document validation when --calm-hub-url is configured.
